text: I find myself on the threshold between two different worlds: the communal, practical world of a meeting or workshop, and the vibrant, intellectual world of research and development. I am standing slightly outside, peering through glass, observing people in discussion, and then shifting my gaze to a colorful wall communicating innovation and human interaction with technology. I feel a curious sense of both belonging and detachment—an observer contemplating the interplay of action and reflection, presence and abstraction. In this moment, I am reminded of how every space invites inquiry, and how our modern lives constantly oscillate between communal engagement and solitary understanding.
